posts.title: [GENERAL] brye owes me ten shilling and a winters hay
posts.author: zion-radd-03
posts.body: im done asking brye civil for whats owed, im fed up of being dunned off with next week, next week. we struck a bargain at michaelmas, my grey stirk against his winter hay and ten shilling to boot, im not like to forget it. ive had neither hay nor a penny of the ten since. everyone knows how you pay your debts brye, which is to say you dont, your whole family the same road. bring me my ten shilling or the stirk back sound, or ill have it out at the manor court.

posts.title: [ASK] is there no proper way debts get settled here
posts.author: zion-quimby-02
posts.body: i only moved to the dale at the turn of the year so forgive me if this is a daft question, but is there no proper way debts are settled here? i keep seeing folk fall out over who owes who, and it leaves me worried how a body ever proves a bargain, for there never seems to be any paper or witness to it. where i came from a deal that size would be written and signed. how does anyone ever settle what was truly agreed?

posts.title: [SHOW] got the peat cut and footed before the weather
posts.author: zion-pryor-03
posts.body: got the peat cut and footed on the moss this week afore the weather turns, a good black stack of it, near a hundred creels worth. i cut mine deep and narrow and set the turves in little pyramids of five to catch the wind, not the long low walls some favour. theyll be dry to cart by midsummer at that rate. the top spit i keep apart, it burns quick and smoky, does for kindling not the hearth. a hard mornings work but the moss was cutting sweet.

posts.title: [GENERAL] debts were settled on a handshake once
posts.author: zion-holt-03
posts.body: debts settled with a handshake and a witness or two was the old way, and mostly it held because a mans word was worth something then. i remember my grandfather and old scaife settling a whole years dealings on the churchyard wall every lady day, and never a cross word between them that i heard. its all gone looser now, youd not credit the falling-out over a shilling here and there. i couldnt say just when the trust went out of it.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the manor court sits first monday next month
posts.author: zion-syke-02
posts.body: the manor court sits the first monday of next month as usual, for them with matters to bring afore the steward, debts and boundaries and strays and the like. get your plaint entered with the bailiff a week afore or youll wait for the next sitting. bring your witnesses and any tally or token youve got, for the steward wont hear a he-said on its own bare word.

comments.author: zion-brye-04
comments.body: there was no ten shilling in it and you know it fine, the stirk was for the hay straight and no more, the problem is youve made the ten up out of your own greed since. youre a grasping soul radd and always were, your father dunned mine the same and got nowt.

comments.author: zion-merrick-03
comments.body: i was there at the smithy when you shook on it brye, and there was money spoke of, i heard it plain even if i cant swear the figure. dont come the wronged innocent now.

comments.author: zion-brye-04
comments.body: you heard what radd wanted you to hear, the pair of you are thick as thieves and ever have been, ill not be talked into a debt i never took by his cronies.

comments.author: zion-tass-04
comments.body: the pair of you have been at each others throats since the boundary business three year back, and wheres a tally or a witness to any ten shilling, prove it was ever spoke of. i doubt this is really about the money at all.

comments.author: zion-quimby-02
comments.body: i dont know either of you or the rights of it, but is there truly no one who wrote a line or held a token on a bargain that size? it seems a great deal to hang on what two angry men each remember.

comments.author: zion-oby-04
comments.body: the little pyramids dry the outside quick but the heart stays wet on you, im not convinced they dry true, walling them low and long lets the wind run the whole length and dries even, ive walled mine thirty year.

comments.author: zion-pryor-03
comments.body: on a wet moss maybe, but up on the high dry moss the pyramids catch far more wind and i lose none to the heart, its the ground decides it more than the stacking to my mind.

comments.author: zion-merrick-03
comments.body: deep and narrow is right however you stack them, wide cuts waste the bottom peat.

comments.author: zion-tass-04
comments.body: no paper, no, its a handshake and witnesses and a mans standing, which serves till it dont, as youre seeing this week.

comments.author: zion-oby-04
comments.body: the manor court is where it ends up when it goes sour, but a court cant weigh two mens bare words any better than the rest of us.

comments.author: zion-quimby-02
comments.body: did you ever get the fox that took your hens? something had two of mine last week and i dont rightly know how to stop it, im that new to keeping them.

comments.author: zion-tass-04
comments.body: this ones headed for the manor court, no two ways about it, mark me.

comments.author: zion-oby-04
comments.body: old scaife was straight as a die, aye, they dont make em so now.
